vehicle.label: Long-Range Surveillance Wing
vehicle.desc: Fixed-wing flying-wing UAV, 2m wingspan, 4+ hour endurance

parts.label: Center Body Pod
parts.spec: Flying-wing center pod, equipment bay door, wing-spar reinforcement, motor pylon at rear

parts.label: Wing Spar (2-piece)
parts.spec: 20mm CFRP main spar, 2-piece centerjoint, anti-rotation key, 800mm half-span each

parts.label: Wing Rib Set x 12
parts.spec: Wing rib NACA airfoil profile, lightening pockets, leading edge sock, trailing edge taper

parts.label: Elevon Servo Pocket x 2
parts.spec: Elevon servo pocket, MG90S or DS215MG, control horn linkage, removable access cover

parts.label: Motor + Pusher Prop Mount
parts.spec: Rear pusher 2814 BLDC mount, 10x7 folding prop clearance, thrust line +1deg down

parts.label: GPS + Pitot Boom
parts.spec: Nose boom 8mm, GPS at tip, pitot static port forward, BEC airflow shielded

parts.label: Camera Gimbal Bay
parts.spec: Forward downward camera gimbal bay 70mm deep, 2-axis micro gimbal, optical window

parts.label: Battery Bay (Slide)
parts.spec: 4S 16000mAh Li-ion slide-in bay, CG adjust slot, top access hatch, balance lead window

parts.label: Integration Service Backbone
parts.spec: Wing/fuselage integration service backbone with assembly_datum pads, harness_raceway, service_hatch, connector_keying, anti-rotation wing datum, and visible load-path inspection marks

requirements: Wing/fuselage integration service backbone with assembly_datum pads, harness_raceway, service_hatch, connector_keying, anti-rotation wing datum, and visible load-path inspection marks

generated_parts.label: Center Body Pod
generated_parts.spec: Flying-wing center pod, equipment bay door, wing-spar reinforcement, motor pylon at rear

requirements: House avionics and equipment in a sealed bay.
Provide structural support for the wing spar and tail boom.
Mount the pusher propeller motor with a cooling path.
Allow access to internal components for maintenance.

verify.value: Parts are integrated only when service access, inspection, and interface clarity remain visible.
verify.rule: good: Parts are integrated only when service access, inspection, and interface clarity remain visible. | bad: Monolithic body hides removable modules, datums, or inspection paths.

verify.value: Fastener patterns, pin joints, slots, bosses, and mating clearances are explicitly represented.
verify.rule: good: Fastener patterns, pin joints, slots, bosses, and mating clearances are explicitly represented. | bad: Joints are described in prose but not represented as holes, bosses, pins, slots, or datums.

verify.value: A reviewer can see what to inspect and why without opening raw JSON.
verify.rule: good: A reviewer can see what to inspect and why without opening raw JSON. | bad: Design has no callouts, access windows, labels, datum pads, or verification focus.

verify.value: Critical joints state locking method (locknut, insert, washer, adhesive note) and torque/preload intent; bolt patterns match load direction.
verify.rule: good: Critical joints state locking method (locknut, insert, washer, adhesive note) and torque/preload intent; bolt patterns match load direction. | bad: Fasteners appear as bare holes with no locking, washer, engagement-length, or preload language anywhere.

verify.value: Threaded connections into printed or soft material use inserts or bosses with adequate engagement depth (~1.5x diameter) and wall around the hole.
verify.rule: good: Threaded connections into printed or soft material use inserts or bosses with adequate engagement depth (~1.5x diameter) and wall around the hole. | bad: Screws thread directly into thin printed walls or blind features with no boss, insert, or depth note.

verify.value: Material/process, wall assumptions, build direction, and supports are explicit enough for design review.
verify.rule: good: Material/process, wall assumptions, build direction, and supports are explicit enough for design review. | bad: Manufacturing process is absent or contradicts geometry.

verify.value: Verify/risk items reference real features or interfaces from part_tree/geometry_ops.
verify.rule: good: Verify/risk items reference real features or interfaces from part_tree/geometry_ops. | bad: Verification is generic pass/warn text not tied to features.

verify.value: Risks cover blocked access, weak interfaces, tolerance mismatch, process limitations, and unsafe overclaiming.
verify.rule: good: Risks cover blocked access, weak interfaces, tolerance mismatch, process limitations, and unsafe overclaiming. | bad: Risks are missing or only mention vague quality concerns.

verify.value: Each stated requirement maps to a verification method (inspection, demonstration, analysis, test) tied to a named feature or interface.
verify.rule: good: Each stated requirement maps to a verification method (inspection, demonstration, analysis, test) tied to a named feature or interface. | bad: Requirements and verification lists exist independently with no traceable pairing.

verify.value: Cross-subsystem interfaces state owner, mating geometry, and what is exchanged (load, power, data, fluid), like a miniature interface control entry.
verify.rule: good: Cross-subsystem interfaces state owner, mating geometry, and what is exchanged (load, power, data, fluid), like a miniature interface control entry. | bad: Subsystems reference each other by name only, with no statement of what crosses the interface.
cad_brief.mass_est_g must be a numeric grams value, and verify must include a mass/budget check.

risk.desc: Bolt preload on motor mount may loosen due to vibration.
risk.mit: Use locking nuts and torque to specification.

risk.desc: Cooling airflow path could be blocked by equipment bay contents.
risk.mit: Design baffle or screen to protect intake.

risk.desc: Access hatch hinges may fatigue over time.
risk.mit: Use robust hinge design and material selection.

physics_paths: Load path from wing spar to motor pylon
Cooling airflow intake to pusher prop
Harness routing for avionics in equipment bay

generated_parts.label: Wing Spar (2-piece)
generated_parts.spec: 20mm CFRP main spar, 2-piece centerjoint, anti-rotation key, 800mm half-span each

requirements: 20mm CFRP main spar, 2-piece centerjoint, anti-rotation key

verify.value: Spar socket faces visible and labeled
verify.rule: Good: Primary locating faces, rail guides, axis rings, spar sockets, or mating pads are visible and labeled. | Bad: Interfaces exist only as abstract neighbor references.

verify.value: Slip fit clearances not explicitly stated for spar halves.
verify.rule: Good: Critical slip, snap, pin, hinge, and slide interfaces state plausible clearances. | Bad: Moving/removable joints have no clearance or fit language.

verify.value: 3 adjacent interfaces: Wing Skin (Left), Wing Skin (Right), Fuselage Frame
verify.rule: Good: Each subsystem has at least three adjacent interfaces or datums. | Bad: Subsystem is isolated and does not show assembly neighbors.

verify.value: Bolted connections use clearance fit, consistent with standard practice.
verify.rule: Good: Fits are named with standard intent: clearance fit for sliding, transition for locating, interference/press only where justified, with a numeric allowance. | Bad: All mating diameters are modeled line-to-line with identical nominal dimensions and no fit class or allowance.

verify.value: M3 bolts used for spar-to-skin connections.
verify.rule: Good: Holes, slots, and pockets match real catalog hardware sizes (M3/M4 clearance holes, standard bearing OD, common pin diameters). | Bad: Hardware features use arbitrary dimensions that match no purchasable fastener, bearing, or pin.

verify.value: Spar socket faces remain accessible.
verify.rule: Good: Spar socket, anti-rotation keys, and center datum remain visible and inspectable. | Bad: Wing/fuselage integration hides spar alignment evidence.

verify.value: Not applicable to wing spar subsystem.
verify.rule: Good: Payload bay is labeled non-weapon/display-only with removable mission/sensor module. | Bad: Design drifts into weaponized or operational payload details.

verify.value: Not applicable to wing spar subsystem.
verify.rule: Good: Battery bay uses slide rail, CG marks, and independent payload/battery removal. | Bad: Battery is buried or coupled to payload in a way that blocks CG adjustment.

verify.value: Loads transfer through spar to carry-through to fuselage frame.
verify.rule: Good: Lift, landing, and payload loads trace through continuous structure: spar to carry-through to fuselage frames, with fittings at every load transfer. | Bad: Structural members end at skins or bays with no fitting, frame, or carry-through; load path breaks at the wing root.

risk.desc: Fatigue failure of spar under repeated loading

_subsystem_plan.subsystem_role: Main spar, ribs, carry-through, fuselage frames; continuous load path
physics_paths: Lift loads from wing skin to spar socket
Landing impact through spar to fuselage frame
Payload weight transfer via carry-through structure

generated_parts.label: Integration Service Backbone
generated_parts.spec: Wing/fuselage integration service backbone with assembly_datum pads, harness_raceway, service_hatch, connector_keying, anti-rotation wing datum, and visible load-path inspection marks

requirements: Wing/fuselage integration service backbone with assembly_datum pads, harness_raceway, service_hatch, connector_keying, anti-rotation wing datum, and visible load-path inspection marks

verify.value: Battery, sensor, actuator, cartridge, payload modules remain removable without full teardown.
verify.rule: [replaceable_module_path] good: Battery, sensor, actuator, cartridge, payload, cable, or joint modules remain removable without full teardown. | bad: Functional modules are fused into a closed body.

verify.value: Harnesses routed through visible raceways.
verify.rule: [cable_harness_visibility] good: Harnesses, tubes, or service channels are routed through visible raceways or protected channels. | bad: Cables are buried inside closed links or shells with no inspection feature.

verify.value: Service hatch provides physical access.
verify.rule: [access_not_just_text] good: Access is represented by geometry: hatch, pocket, channel, fastener pattern, slot, window, or removable cover. | bad: Access is claimed only in prose.

verify.value: Harness exits have strain relief and respect bend radius.
verify.rule: [harness_strain_relief_and_bend_radius] good: Every cable exit shows strain relief geometry (clamp, grommet, tie point) and routing respects a stated minimum bend radius away from moving joints and sharp edges. | bad: Cables exit bare holes, cross hinge lines unsupported, or bend around sharp printed edges with no relief feature.

verify.value: Connectors reachable with finger/tool clearance and keying.
verify.rule: [connector_accessibility_and_keying] good: Connectors are reachable without disassembling structure, have finger/tool clearance, and mention keying or labeling to prevent mis-mating. | bad: Connectors face closed walls, sit in unreachable pockets, or identical adjacent connectors have no keying or label.

verify.value: Primary datum pads are visible and labeled.
verify.rule: [datum_visibility] good: Primary locating faces, rail guides, axis rings, spar sockets, or mating pads are visible and labeled. | bad: Interfaces exist only as abstract neighbor references.

verify.value: Critical slip, snap, pin interfaces state plausible clearances.
verify.rule: [clearance_named] good: Critical slip, snap, pin, hinge, and slide interfaces state plausible clearances. | bad: Moving/removable joints have no clearance or fit language.

verify.value: Subsystem has 3 adjacent interfaces.
verify.rule: [interface_count] good: Each subsystem has at least three adjacent interfaces or datums. | bad: Subsystem is isolated and does not show assembly neighbors.

verify.value: Fits are named with standard intent (clearance, transition).
verify.rule: [standard_fit_vocabulary] good: Fits are named with standard intent: clearance fit for sliding, transition for locating, interference/press only where justified, with a numeric allowance. | bad: All mating diameters are modeled line-to-line with identical nominal dimensions and no fit class or allowance.

verify.value: Holes match real catalog hardware sizes.
verify.rule: [standard_hardware_dimensioning] good: Holes, slots, and pockets match real catalog hardware sizes (M3/M4 clearance holes, standard bearing OD, common pin diameters). | bad: Hardware features use arbitrary dimensions that match no purchasable fastener, bearing, or pin.

verify.value: Material and process are explicit.
verify.rule: [wall_process_readiness] good: Material/process, wall assumptions, build direction, and supports are explicit enough for design review. | bad: Manufacturing process is absent or contradicts geometry.

verify.value: Estimated mass is 250g, target was 200g.
verify.rule: cad_brief.mass_est_g must be a numeric grams value, and verify must include a mass/budget check.

risk.desc: Connector mis-mating due to insufficient keying or labeling.
risk.mit: Implement robust connector keying and clear labeling.

risk.desc: Cable chafing or damage at sharp edges or tight bends.
risk.mit: Ensure strain relief features are present and routing respects bend radius.

physics_paths: Harness Raceway: Routes cables from fuselage to wingtip.
Service Hatch Access: Allows access to internal components for maintenance.
Load Path Inspection Marks: Visible markings along the primary load path for structural verification.
integration_opportunities: Co-location of sensors and data connectors.

manufacturing_strategy.datum: Primary datum pad on fuselage interface
manufacturing_strategy.support: Required for overhangs and internal features

verification_focus: Harness strain relief and bend radius.
